title: Display Format
text: Display Format Specify a display format for field layout, which can be used to format the data for display or string substitution. DIS ( Format ) For Numeric, Float, Date, Time, or Datetime, you can specify an NLS format: DIS ( $NLS ( NlsFormat )) Example: DIS(True/False) for Boolean data where the actual values are 1 and 0 Example: DIS(AA*, dd-MMM*-yyyy) for a Date field Technical Name: FORMAT Expected Value: String of up to 32 characters. Supports Unicode. Default Value: None Arguments Argument Description Format Code or pattern that defines the formatting applied to the data. The available display formats depend on the data type. For more information, see: Display Formats for Boolean Data Display Formats for Strings Display Formats for Numeric and Float Data Display Formats for Date and Time Data NlsFormat Keyword or display format pattern that defines the NLS formatting to be applied to the data; one of $NLS(NUMBER) , $NLS(FLOAT) or $NLS( Format ) . By default, if $NLS_LOCALE is specified in the assignment file, or in ProcScript (using $nlslocale and $webinfo("locale") ), the language and styling conventions of the locale are applied. You can override this default behavior using $NLS( NlsFormat ) . For more information, see NLS Display Formats . Description Display formats apply a pattern to the data to control how it is formatted for display. They can be specified in the Field Layout property, and can also be controlled using the $format ProcScript function. For Numeric, Float, Date, Time, or Datetime fields, you can browse for a prefedined display format. The available display formats depend on the data type. For more information, see: Display Formats for Boolean Data Display Formats for Strings Display Formats for Numeric and Float Data Display Formats for Date and Time Data Applies to Field Layout Modeled Field Layout Related Links Field Layout Codes Field layout codes specify how data is formatted for display. When multiple codes are specified, they must be separated by commas. $format Return the field data, formatted according to the display format (DIS). Language and Locale The language of an application is associated not only with a character set, but with regional conventions for sorting, for expressing numbers, currency, date and time, and so on. The combination of language and region is known as locale. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
